Patient experience.

Measure This hospital Network avg Δ
HCAHPS overall star rating 4/5 2.9/5 +1.1
Would definitely recommend 79% 70.3% +9pp
Hospital rating 9 or 10 of 10 76% 69.2% +7pp
Nurses always communicated well 81% 75.9% +5pp
Doctors always communicated well 80% 76.9% +3pp
Given clear info about recovery 87% 84.8% +2pp
Room and bathroom always clean 72% 68.6% +3pp
Staff always explained meds 63% 57.8% +5pp
Quiet at night, always 51% 52.6% -2pp

About this price.

This price comes directly from Beth Israel Deaconess Medical Center's machine-readable file (MRF), which the Hospital Price Transparency Rule (45 CFR 180.50) requires every US hospital to publish. We download it, parse the row for CPT 83036, and show you the dollar amount as published.

The cash-pay number is what an uninsured patient would be charged. Insurance-negotiated rates vary by payer and plan — see the table above for the range, or use the comparison tool for plan-specific numbers.

For a national view of hemoglobin a1c prices, see the Hemoglobin A1c overview.

Data sources: CMS Hospital Price Transparency rule (45 CFR 180.50). CMS Hospital Care Compare (xubh-q36u). Last refresh: 2026-04-28.
A consumer reading of CMS-mandated MRF data. Not medical or financial advice. Itemized · itemized.health
